story.iWant: a comprehensive automated test suite that validates conversion accuracy against all 1,501 sample files
story.soThat: I can ensure 95%+ conversion fidelity, detect regressions immediately, and maintain confidence in the conversion engine as features are added
story.tasks:
### Task 1: Create Test File Structure (AC-6) - Create `internal/formats/np3/np3_test.go` with table-driven pattern - Create `internal/formats/xmp/xmp_test.go` with table-driven pattern - Create `internal/formats/lrtemplate/lrtemplate_test.go` with table-driven pattern - Extract `validateRecipe()` helper function ### Task 2: Implement Round-Trip Tests (AC-2) - Create `internal/converter/roundtrip_test.go` - Implement `TestRoundTrip_NP3_XMP_NP3` with tolerance ±1 - Implement `TestRoundTrip_XMP_lrtemplate_XMP` - Implement `TestRoundTrip_NP3_lrtemplate_NP3` - Extract `compareRecipes()` helper function ### Task 3: Implement Error Path Tests (AC-5) - Add `TestParseInvalidMagic` to each format test file - Add `TestParseTruncatedFile` to each format test file - Add `TestParseOutOfRangeParameter` tests - Verify error messages and ConversionError wrapping ### Task 4: Configure Coverage Reporting (AC-3) - Add coverage files to `.gitignore` - Add `test`, `coverage`, `coverage-html` targets to Makefile - Document coverage workflow in README ### Task 5: Optimize Test Performance (AC-7) - Enable `t.Parallel()` in test files - Benchmark test suite execution - Verify <10 second total runtime
### Task 6: Create Test Utilities Package (AC-6) - Create `internal/testutil/helpers.go` - Implement `CopyFile()` helper - Implement `CreateTempFile()` helper
### Task 7: Validate All Acceptance Criteria - Run full test suite and verify all 1,501 files tested (AC-1) - Verify coverage ≥90% for all packages (AC-3) - Verify round-trip accuracy ≥95% (AC-2) - Verify test suite <10 seconds (AC-7)
### Task 8: Update Documentation - Update README.md with Testing section - Add CONTRIBUTING.md section on testing requirements - Document test patterns in code comments

story-context.acceptanceCriteria: ### AC-1: Complete Sample File Coverage ✅ All 22 NP3 files parse successfully (100% success rate) ✅ All 913 XMP files parse successfully (100% success rate) ✅ All 566 lrtemplate files parse successfully (100% success rate) ✅ Each file tested as individual subtest (1,501 subtests visible in output) ✅ Test suite completes in <10 seconds ### AC-2: Round-Trip Conversion Validation ✅ NP3 → XMP → NP3 produces functionally identical output (tolerance ±1 for rounding) ✅ XMP → lrtemplate → XMP preserves all critical parameters (Exposure, Contrast, Saturation, HSL) ✅ lrtemplate → NP3 → lrtemplate maintains parameter accuracy ≥95% ✅ All 6 conversion paths tested (NP3↔XMP, NP3↔lrtemplate, XMP↔lrtemplate) ✅ Failed round-trips report specific parameter mismatches with expected vs actual values ### AC-3: Test Coverage Metrics ✅ Overall test coverage ≥90% ✅ `internal/converter` package coverage ≥90% ✅ `internal/formats/np3` package coverage ≥90% ✅ `internal/formats/xmp` package coverage ≥90% ✅ `internal/formats/lrtemplate` package coverage ≥90% ✅ Coverage report exported to `coverage.out` and `coverage.html` ✅ Uncovered lines documented with justification (if any) ### AC-4: Parameter Range Validation ✅ All parameters fall within expected ranges ✅ Contrast: -100 to +100 (or format-specific range) ✅ Saturation: -100 to +100 ✅ Exposure: -5.0 to +5.0 ✅ Sharpness: 0 to 150 ✅ HSL Hue/Saturation/Luminance: -100 to +100 ✅ Out-of-range values trigger test failure with specific value logged ### AC-5: Error Path Testing ✅ Invalid magic bytes trigger clear error (e.g., "invalid NP3 magic: expected 'NP', got 'XX'") ✅ Truncated files trigger size validation error ✅ Malformed XML (XMP) triggers parse error with line number ✅ Invalid Lua syntax (lrtemplate) triggers syntax error ✅ All errors wrapped in `ConversionError` type with operation/format context ### AC-6: Test Organization and Structure ✅ Each format package has test file: `np3_test.go`, `xmp_test.go`, `lrtemplate_test.go` ✅ Converter package has: `converter_test.go`, `roundtrip_test.go` ✅ Tests follow table-driven pattern (loop over `testdata/` files) ✅ Helper functions extracted: `validateRecipe()`, `compareRecipes()`, `copyFile()` ✅ Test fixtures organized: `testdata/{np3,xmp,lrtemplate}/` subdirectories ✅ Consistent test naming: `TestParse`, `TestGenerate`, `TestRoundTrip__` ### AC-7: Fast Test Execution ✅ Total execution time <10 seconds ✅ Format parser tests: <3 seconds (1,501 files) ✅ Round-trip tests: <4 seconds ✅ No slow tests blocking developer workflow ✅ Parallel execution enabled (`go test -parallel=8`) ### AC-8: CI/CD Integration Foundation ✅ Tests runnable via `go test ./...` (no special setup) ✅ Coverage report generated via `go test -coverprofile=coverage.out ./...` ✅ Exit code 0 on success, 1 on failure (standard Go behavior) ✅ Output parseable by CI tools (TAP/JUnit format not required for MVP) ✅ No environment-specific dependencies (runs on Linux, macOS, Windows)

doc.title: Product Requirements Document
doc.snippet: 99.9% successful conversions on valid input files, graceful error handling with clear user messaging, automated validation against 1,501 test files

doc.title: Product Requirements Document
doc.snippet: Table-driven tests using 1,501 real sample files, round-trip validation (A → B → A produces identical output), 95%+ accuracy validation, 90%+ test coverage for core conversion logic

doc.title: Architecture Document
doc.snippet: Table-driven tests with 1,501 real sample files from testdata/, round-trip validation (A → B → A produces identical output), comprehensive coverage of all format parsers, validates 95%+ accuracy goal

doc.title: Epic 6 Technical Specification - Validation & Testing
doc.snippet: 100% parse success on all 1,501 valid sample files, test suite completes in <10 seconds, all format parsers have test files, 1,501 individual subtests visible

doc.title: Epic 6 Technical Specification - Validation & Testing
doc.snippet: NP3 → XMP → NP3 produces functionally identical output (tolerance ±1), all 6 conversion paths tested, failed round-trips report specific parameter mismatches

doc.title: Epic 6 Technical Specification - Validation & Testing
doc.snippet: Overall coverage ≥90%, core packages ≥90%, coverage exported to coverage.out and coverage.html

doc.title: Epic 6 Technical Specification - Validation & Testing
doc.snippet: Test pyramid: 70% unit tests (format parsers, 1,501 files), 25% integration tests (round-trip conversions), 5% manual tests. Go testing package with table-driven tests, subtests for granular reporting, coverage via go test -cover

artifact.reason: Main conversion API that will be tested by all test suites
Existing test pattern showing table-driven approach and file discovery pattern
Error type that must be validated in error path tests (AC-5)
Existing validation test patterns that can be reused for parameter range validation (AC-4)
NP3 format parser/generator that needs comprehensive test coverage
XMP format parser/generator that needs comprehensive test coverage
lrtemplate format parser/generator that needs comprehensive test coverage
913 XMP sample files for table-driven testing (AC-1)
22 NP3 sample files for table-driven testing (AC-1)
566 lrtemplate sample files for table-driven testing (AC-1)
Will be updated to add coverage targets (AC-3, Task 4)

stdlib.package: Test framework, subtests, parallel execution, benchmarking
File I/O for reading test fixtures
Glob pattern matching for test file discovery

external.package: CLI framework (already installed, not needed for tests)

story-context.constraints: - **Zero External Test Dependencies**: Use only Go standard library `testing` package (no testify, gomega, ginkgo, etc.) - **Table-Driven Pattern Required**: All format parser tests must loop over sample files from testdata/examples directories - **Subtest Isolation**: Each sample file must be tested in a separate subtest for granular failure reporting - **Performance Requirement**: Complete test suite must finish in <10 seconds (AC-7) - **Parallel Execution**: Tests must support `t.Parallel()` for concurrent execution - **Coverage Threshold**: ≥90% coverage for internal/converter and internal/formats packages (AC-3) - **Round-Trip Tolerance**: Parameter comparison must use ±1 tolerance for rounding errors (AC-2) - **Error Message Clarity**: All error path tests must verify specific error messages mention the failure cause - **ConversionError Wrapping**: All format parsing errors must be wrapped in ConversionError type (AC-5) - **File Organization**: Test files must follow Go convention: {package}_test.go alongside source files (AC-6) - **No Test Data Modification**: Sample files in testdata/ and examples/ are immutable, version controlled - **Cross-Platform**: Tests must run on Linux, macOS, Windows without environment-specific setup (AC-8)

interface.name: Convert API
interface.signature: func Convert(input []byte, fromFormat, toFormat string) ([]byte, error)

interface.name: ConversionError Type
interface.signature: type ConversionError struct { Operation string; Format string; Cause error }

interface.name: Go Testing Framework
interface.signature: func TestXxx(t *testing.T), t.Run(name, func), t.Parallel(), t.Error/Fatal/Log

tests.standards:
**Go Standard Testing Framework:** - Use `testing` package from Go stdlib (no external test frameworks) - Table-driven tests for all format parsers (loop over sample files) - Subtests for granular failure reporting (`t.Run()`) - Parallel execution enabled (`t.Parallel()`) for fast test runs - Helper functions marked with `t.Helper()` for cleaner stack traces
**Test Organization:** - Test files named `{package}_test.go` (e.g., `np3_test.go`) - Tests in same package as code under test (white-box testing) - Helper functions extracted to reduce duplication (`validateRecipe()`, `compareRecipes()`) - Test utilities in `internal/testutil/helpers.go`
**Coverage Standards:** - Overall coverage ≥90% (enforced by CI/CD in Story 6-7) - Core packages (converter, formats/*) ≥90% each - Coverage reports generated via `go test -coverprofile=coverage.out` - HTML reports for line-by-line coverage inspection
**Assertion Patterns:** - Use `t.Error()` for non-fatal failures (continue test) - Use `t.Fatal()` for fatal failures (stop test immediately) - Clear error messages with context (expected vs actual, file path, parameter name)
**Test Isolation:** - Each test independent (no shared state between tests) - Temporary files cleaned up via `t.TempDir()` - No test order dependencies (tests run in any order)
tests.locations: - `internal/converter/converter_test.go` - Existing conversion path tests - `internal/converter/roundtrip_test.go` - NEW: Round-trip validation tests (AC-2) - `internal/formats/np3/np3_test.go` - NEW: NP3 parser/generator tests (AC-1) - `internal/formats/xmp/xmp_test.go` - NEW: XMP parser/generator tests (AC-1) - `internal/formats/lrtemplate/lrtemplate_test.go` - NEW: lrtemplate parser/generator tests (AC-1) - `internal/models/recipe_test.go` - Existing model validation tests - `internal/testutil/helpers.go` - NEW: Shared test utilities (AC-6) - `testdata/xmp/` - 913 XMP sample files - `examples/np3/` - 22 NP3 sample files - `examples/lrtemplate/` - 566 lrtemplate sample files
tests.ideas:
**AC-1 Test Ideas (Complete Sample File Coverage):** - Create `TestParse()` in each format test file that globs all sample files from testdata/examples - Each sample file tested as individual subtest for granular reporting - Verify parse succeeds (no error) for all valid files - Call `validateRecipe()` helper to check parameter ranges (AC-4) - Track test count to verify all 1,501 files are discovered and tested **AC-2 Test Ideas (Round-Trip Conversion Validation):** - Create `TestRoundTrip_NP3_XMP_NP3()` that parses NP3 → converts to XMP → parses XMP → converts back to NP3 → compares original vs final - Implement `compareRecipes()` helper that compares all critical parameters with tolerance ±1 - Report specific parameter mismatches (expected vs actual, parameter name, tolerance) - Test all 6 conversion paths (NP3↔XMP, NP3↔lrtemplate, XMP↔lrtemplate) - Use subset of sample files for round-trip (not all 1,501, maybe 10-20 representative files per format) **AC-3 Test Ideas (Test Coverage Metrics):** - Add `coverage` target to Makefile: `go test -coverprofile=coverage.out ./...` - Add `coverage-html` target: `go tool cover -html=coverage.out -o coverage.html` - Add coverage files to `.gitignore` (coverage.out, coverage.html) - Document coverage workflow in README (how to run, interpret results) **AC-4 Test Ideas (Parameter Range Validation):** - Implement `validateRecipe(t *testing.T, recipe *model.UniversalRecipe)` helper - Check all critical parameters: Contrast (-100 to +100), Saturation (-100 to +100), Exposure (-5.0 to +5.0), Sharpness (0 to 150), HSL (-100 to +100) - Use `t.Errorf()` with specific parameter name and value when out of range - Called from all format parser tests (AC-1) **AC-5 Test Ideas (Error Path Testing):** - `TestParseInvalidMagic()`: Create file with wrong magic bytes, verify error message mentions "invalid magic" - `TestParseTruncatedFile()`: Create file with only header, verify error mentions "too small" or "truncated" - `TestParseMalformedXML()`: Create invalid XML, verify parse error with line number (if applicable) - `TestParseInvalidLua()`: Create invalid Lua syntax, verify syntax error - Verify all errors are wrapped in `ConversionError` type with correct Operation and Format fields **AC-6 Test Ideas (Test Organization and Structure):** - Create test files following pattern: np3_test.go, xmp_test.go, lrtemplate_test.go - Extract helper functions: validateRecipe(), compareRecipes(), copyFile() - Create testutil package with common utilities - Verify consistent naming: TestParse, TestGenerate, TestRoundTrip_* **AC-7 Test Ideas (Fast Test Execution):** - Add `t.Parallel()` to top-level test functions - Add `t.Parallel()` to subtests for concurrent execution - Benchmark test suite: `time go test ./...` and verify <10 seconds - Profile slow tests and optimize if needed
**AC-8 Test Ideas (CI/CD Integration Foundation):** - Verify `go test ./...` runs all tests (no special setup) - Verify exit code 0 on success, 1 on failure - Verify coverage report generated: `go test -coverprofile=coverage.out ./...` - Ensure no environment-specific dependencies (cross-platform compatibility)
